definition.description: Full-width banner with background image or video, overlay heading, body text, and optional CTA button.

cells: Background media: image (picture element) or video URL
rows.notes: For video variant, author pastes an MP4 URL. For image variant, author inserts a picture.

cells: Overlay content: h1 heading, paragraph text, optional CTA link (bold link = primary button)
rows.notes: All overlay content in a single cell. Bold link renders as primary CTA button.

variantNotes.default: Hero block table header is just 'Hero'. Background image with text overlay.
variantNotes.video: Hero (video) — background autoplays muted MP4 video. Image serves as poster/fallback.

css.overlayGradient: linear-gradient with semi-transparent dark layer for text readability

js.videoAutoplay: Muted autoplay with poster image fallback (homepage only)
js.lazyLoad: Eager load for LCP — hero is always in first section

definition.description: Grid of treatment area cards with image, title, description, and link. One row per card.

rows.notes: Each row is one card. Image in col 1, all text content in col 2.

definition.example:
| Cards |
| --- | --- |
| ![lips](/images/lips.jpg) | **Lippen** Mooie lippen in alle vormen... [Lees meer](/nl/treatment/lips) |

css.layout: CSS Grid, 2-3 columns on desktop, 1 column mobile
css.cardStyle: Image top, text below, rounded corners, subtle shadow

js.notes: Minimal JS — decoration only, no interactivity needed

definition.description: Rotating slideshow with navigation. Each row is one slide containing image and/or text content.

cells: Slide content: image + text, or image-only, or text-only
rows.notes: Single-column collection. Each row = one slide.

variantNotes.default: Standard carousel with dots/arrows, 1 visible slide.
variantNotes.products: Carousel (products) — shows 3-4 items visible at once with prev/next arrows. Used for product showcase sections.

js.library: Tiny Slider (tns) pattern from Block Collection carousel
js.responsive: 1 item mobile, 3-4 items desktop for products variant

definition.description: Expandable Q&A sections. Each row is one item: col 1 = question/label, col 2 = answer/body.

rows.notes: Two-column collection. First column = clickable header, second = expandable body.

css.icon: Chevron/plus toggle icon on right side of header

js.behavior: Click to toggle, one open at a time (optional)

definition.description: Side-by-side content layout. Single row with 2 or 3 columns.

rows.notes: 2 columns for media-text layouts. 3 columns for 3-up grids. Column order determines LTR/RTL appearance.

variantNotes.default: 2-column layout. Image in one column, text content in the other. Author controls order by placing image left or right.
variantNotes.3-col: Columns (3-col) — three equal columns, each with image + text. Used for product info grids on treatment pages.

css.layout: CSS Grid or Flexbox, equal or auto-sized columns

definition.description: Tabbed content panels auto-blocked from consecutive sections. Each section with an H2 becomes a tab panel; the H2 text becomes the tab label.
definition.autoBlockingRule: Sections within a tabs-container section. Section metadata style=tabs triggers auto-blocking. Each H2 heading = tab label.

rows.notes: Each row = one tab. Col 1 = label text, col 2 = panel content. Alternatively auto-blocked from sections.

css.tabBar: Horizontal tab buttons, active state with bottom border accent color
css.panel: Content area below tab bar, smooth show/hide

js.behavior: Click tab to switch panel, URL hash update for deep linking
js.a11y: ARIA tablist/tab/tabpanel roles, keyboard arrow navigation

definition.description: Image comparison slider showing before/after treatment results. Each row = one comparison pair.

rows.notes: Two-column collection. Each row is a pair. Heading/disclaimer text goes in a preceding default content section.

variantNotes.default: Single comparison pair with draggable slider divider.
variantNotes.carousel: Before-after (carousel) — multiple pairs in a carousel with dot navigation. Used on homepage.

css.slider: Vertical divider line with drag handle
css.labels: Before/After text labels on each side

js.interaction: Drag slider to reveal before/after. Touch and mouse support.
js.component: Custom implementation using img-comparison-slider Web Component pattern or pointer events.
js.a11y: Keyboard accessible slider (arrow keys), appropriate ARIA roles

definition.description: Clinic search widget with Google Maps Places autocomplete. Configuration block with key-value rows.

cells: Vind een kliniek bij jou in de buurt

rows.notes: Reference to env config, not hardcoded
URL to redirect for full results (compact variant only)

variantNotes.compact: Clinic-finder (compact) — inline search bar with heading, used on treatment pages. Redirects to full results page.
variantNotes.full-page: Clinic-finder (full-page) — full search with map, list view, and detailed clinic cards.

css.compact: Centered search bar with gradient background section
css.fullPage: Split layout: map on one side, results list on other

js.googleMaps: Places Autocomplete API for address search
js.geolocation: Browser geolocation API for 'use my location' feature
js.iclFeed: Integration with Allergan ICL clinic feed for results

definition.description: Video embed auto-blocked from Vimeo URLs. Author simply pastes a Vimeo link as default content; the embed block auto-detects and wraps it.
definition.autoBlockingRule: Any standalone link to vimeo.com is auto-blocked into an embed block.

rows.notes: Single cell with the video URL. Block Collection embed handles oEmbed resolution.

js.oEmbed: Fetches Vimeo oEmbed data to create responsive iframe
js.lazyLoad: Load iframe only when scrolled into view

definition.description: Interactive face image with positioned markers linking to treatment areas. Custom block — must be built from scratch.

rows.notes: Row 1: The background image with treatment area markers overlaid.
Row 2+: Each row defines one marker. Label is the treatment area name, link URL is the target page. Coordinates are determined by CSS positioning per marker index or authored as percentages.

definition.alternativeModel: Could also be modeled as a single-cell block with a list of links, where JS positions markers based on link text matching predefined coordinate data.

css.markers: Circular pulsing markers with tooltip labels on hover

js.interaction: Hover to show label, click to navigate to treatment page

definition.description: Sticky horizontal anchor navigation for in-page sections. Author creates a list of anchor links.

cells: Unordered list of anchor links: e.g., - [Over JUVÉDERM®](#about-juvederm)\n- [Veiligheid](#side-effects)\n...
rows.notes: Single cell with a bulleted list of links. Each link points to a section ID on the same page.

css.activeState: Underline/highlight for the currently-visible section's link

js.scrollSpy: IntersectionObserver to detect which section is in view and highlight corresponding link
js.smoothScroll: Click link to smooth-scroll to target section
js.horizontalScroll: Scroll the active link into view on mobile

homepage.description: Main landing page with video hero, brand statement, scroll-reveal cards, before/after carousel, treatment areas (tabs+cards+image-map), clinic finder CTA, and footnotes.

sections.name: hero
sections.notes: Full-bleed video hero with autoplay MP4 background, heading 'Jouw unieke schoonheid', and CTA.

sections.name: brand-statement
sections.notes: Centered brand statement text with supporting lifestyle image. No block needed — default content with section style.

sections.name: key-features
sections.notes: Three columns with icon + stat + description (natural results, satisfaction, longevity, calendar). Originally a scroll-reveal animation — simplified to columns in EDS.

sections.name: footnotes-1
sections.notes: Legal footnote references for the statistics section above.

sections.name: before-after
sections.notes: Heading text followed by carousel of 3 before/after comparison pairs.

sections.name: treatment-areas
sections.notes: Tabbed section with image-map and treatment cards. Tab 1 = face image map, Tab 2+ = card grids. Complex section — may simplify to cards-only in EDS.

sections.name: clinic-finder-cta
sections.notes: Compact clinic search with video/image background and 'Vind een kliniek' heading.

sections.name: footnotes-2
sections.notes: Page-level legal references and disclaimers.

metadata.description: Juvéderm® facial fillers help to enhance, restore or hydrate your features.

treatment-page.description: Treatment detail pages (lips, eye-area, enhance, restore, male) with hero, media-text intro, 3-up product info, before/after comparison, product showcase carousel, FAQ accordion, treatment area cards, clinic finder CTA, and optional video embed.

sections.name: hero
sections.notes: Treatment-specific hero image with h1 heading and optional subtext.

sections.name: intro-media-text
sections.notes: Image on one side, introductory paragraph on the other. RTL or LTR depending on the treatment page.

sections.name: product-info
sections.notes: Three product/benefit columns with icons and descriptions.

sections.name: footnotes-1
sections.notes: Footnotes for product claims.

sections.name: before-after
sections.notes: Heading, description, and single before/after comparison slider.

sections.name: product-showcase
sections.notes: Heading followed by multi-item product carousel (4 visible on desktop).

sections.name: video-embed
sections.notes: Optional — only on lips and eye-area pages. Vimeo video embed.

sections.name: faq-short
sections.notes: 3-5 FAQ items relevant to this treatment area.

sections.name: treatment-areas
sections.notes: Cross-links to other treatment area pages as card grid.

sections.name: clinic-finder-cta
sections.notes: Compact clinic finder search bar.

sections.name: footnotes-2
sections.notes: Page-level disclaimers.

faq.description: FAQ/QA page with hero, sticky topic menu, multiple Q&A sections with accordions, media-text blocks, key takeaways, and footnotes.

sections.name: hero
sections.notes: Hero with h1 'Ontdek het antwoord op je vraag'.

sections.name: topic-navigation
sections.notes: Sticky horizontal anchor nav with 5 topic links.

sections.name: qa-section-about
sections.notes: About JUVÉDERM® section with intro media-text, Q&A accordion, and footnotes. Section ID: about-juvederm.

sections.name: key-takeaways
sections.notes: Three highlight items side by side with icons and text.

sections.name: qa-section-safety
sections.notes: Safety section. Section ID: side-effects.

sections.name: qa-section-expectations
sections.notes: What to expect section. Section ID: expectation.

sections.name: qa-section-results
sections.notes: Results and costs section. Section ID: duration.

sections.name: qa-section-facial-areas
sections.notes: Facial areas section. Section ID: facial-areas.

sections.name: clinic-finder-cta
sections.notes: Compact clinic finder CTA.

sections.name: footnotes
sections.notes: Page-level references.

metadata.description: JUVÉDERM® is a collection of injectable facial fillers...

clinic-finder.description: Full clinic search page with hero and full-page clinic finder widget.

sections.name: hero
sections.notes: Hero with 'Vind een kliniek' heading.

sections.name: clinic-search
sections.notes: Full clinic search with map, list toggle, and detailed results.

metadata.description: Find the right clinic for you

legal.description: Simple legal/disclaimer page with default content only.

sections.name: content
sections.notes: Pure text content — headings and paragraphs. No blocks needed.

contact.description: Contact page with heading and contact information.

sections.name: content
sections.notes: Contact details as default content. reCAPTCHA form handled via delayed.js integration if needed.

informational.description: General terms and conditions page — legal text content.

sections.name: content
sections.notes: Same pattern as legal archetype. Plain text content.

imageHandling.heroDesktop: Download from data-cmp-src, convert to WebP
imageHandling.brandImage: Download lifestyle image from brand statement section

specialHandling: Video hero: Extract MP4 source URL from video source element
Scroll-reveal section: Simplify to static 3-col columns block (animation not critical)
BA carousel: Extract all 3 comparison pairs from carousel slides
Treatment area tabs: Flatten if image-map is deferred; otherwise keep tabs structure

imageHandling.heroImages: Download desktop + mobile crops from data-cmp-src URLs

specialHandling: 3-up section uses emu-carousel with data-items=3 in static mode — extract as 3 columns block
Product showcase carousel: Extract individual product items as carousel slides
Vimeo embeds: Extract video ID, create plain Vimeo URL for auto-blocking
Media-text RTL/LTR: Column order in block table determines layout direction
Topic menu: Extract anchor links from nav ul, map to section IDs
Multiple QA sections: Each .qa-section becomes an EDS section with section metadata ID
Key takeaways: Extract 3 items from static carousel, model as columns (3-col)
Accordion within QA containers: Extract question from header button text, answer from body content
Google Maps API key must be migrated to environment config
ICL feed integration requires backend proxy — document as Phase 3+ work
Three URL variants (/find-a-clinic, /clinics, /clinic) should be consolidated to one page with redirects

contentSelectors.content: main .aaaem-container p, main .aaaem-container h1, main .aaaem-container h2

specialHandling: Pure text extraction — no blocks needed
reCAPTCHA-protected form — may need to convert to a simple mailto link or external form service integration
Pure text extraction — no blocks needed

ctaPattern.primary: Bold link text renders as filled gradient button (purple/magenta). Used for 'Vind een kliniek', 'Lees meer'.
ctaPattern.secondary: Regular link text renders as outlined/bordered button. Used for secondary actions.
ctaPattern.convention: Author creates **[CTA Text](url)** for primary CTA, plain [CTA Text](url) for secondary.

imageNotes.format: Download originals, serve as WebP via EDS automatic optimization
imageNotes.responsive: EDS handles responsive images via picture element and width parameters
imageNotes.heroImages: Separate desktop and mobile crops where available

custom.name: Google Maps Places API
custom.approach: Load in clinic-finder block JS only, consider server proxy

blocksToCreate.name: image-map
blocksToCreate.notes: Interactive face image with positioned markers. No Block Collection equivalent. Must be custom built with percentage-based positioning and responsive recalculation.

blocksToAdd.name: embed
blocksToAdd.notes: Only used on 2 treatment pages (lips, eye-area). Copy from Block Collection.

duplicatePages.homepage: https://www.juvederm.nl/ and /nl are the same — use /nl as canonical, redirect / to /nl
duplicatePages.clinicFinder: /nl/find-a-clinic, /nl/clinics, /nl/clinic are same page — use /nl/find-a-clinic as canonical, redirect others
